context: Checks whether the final artifact follows the phoenix-authorization-patterns instructions from the published Elixir Phoenix Skills plugin.

checklist.name: instruction-1
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Always authorize on the server in event handlers** — never rely on UI-only checks

checklist.name: instruction-2
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Verify resource ownership by comparing `current_scope.user.id` against the resource's `user_id`** — never trust client-sent user IDs

checklist.name: instruction-3
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use policy modules for complex authorization** — don't inline permission checks in LiveViews or controllers

checklist.name: instruction-4
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Define policy module rules** — add clauses for every action (`view`, `edit`, `delete`, etc.) before wiring up any LiveView

checklist.name: instruction-5
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Write unauthorized-path tests first** — confirm that a non-owner receives an error and that the mutation does not occur
